question_text: In the diagram shown below, a person pushes a box. The applied force is horizontal and points east while the displacement is upward. What is the sign of the work done by the applied force?

answer_key.explanation: When the force is perpendicular to the displacement, the work done by that force is zero.
common_mistakes: Assuming non-zero work whenever a force is applied.

visual.caption: Force and displacement arrows on a box.
visual.altText: A box with one horizontal arrow representing applied force and a separate vertical arrow representing displacement direction.
